message:
Implement hard kill fallback using kill -9 (bd-bh74z)

Add hard kill fallback for --robot-smart-restart when soft exit fails.
This provides a more aggressive exit method when Ctrl+C doesn't work.

Changes:
- Add HardKillResult struct to track kill operation details
- Add hardKillAgent() function that:
  - Gets shell PID from tmux list-panes
  - Gets child PID using existing getChildPID (reuses status_enrich.go)
  - Sends kill -9 to child process
  - Returns detailed result for debugging
- Add killProcess() and getShellPID() helpers in exit_sequences.go
- Add splitBySpace() helper for parsing tmux output
- Update SmartRestartOptions with HardKill and HardKillOnly flags
- Update RestartSequence with HardKillUsed and HardKillResult fields
- Update executeRestart() to:
  - Try soft exit first (unless HardKillOnly)
  - Fall back to hard kill if soft exit fails and HardKill is enabled
  - Return structured errors with full context for failures
- Add comprehensive unit tests for new functionality

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1gkal","title":"Implement bulk prompt delivery","description":"Implement bulk prompt delivery.

## Functionality

Send assignment prompts to multiple panes efficiently.

## Default Prompt Template

```
Read AGENTS.md, register with Agent Mail. Work on: {bead_id} - {bead_title}.
Use br show {bead_id} for details. Mark in_progress when starting. Use ultrathink.
```

## Template Variables

- {bead_id}: The bead ID
- {bead_title}: Bead title/description
- {bead_type}: task, feature, bug, etc.
- {bead_deps}: Dependencies list
- {session}: Session name
- {pane}: Pane number

## Delivery Strategy

Option A: Sequential (safer)
- Send to pane 2, wait 1s
- Send to pane 3, wait 1s
- etc.

Option B: Parallel (faster)
- Send to all panes simultaneously
- May overwhelm if too many

Recommend: Sequential by default, --parallel flag for advanced users.

## Custom Templates

--prompt-template=<FILE> for custom prompts:
```
Custom marching orders here.
Bead: {bead_id}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:28.015184270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:09:42.752527055Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:09:42.751110447Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gkal","depends_on_id":"bd-t9qp4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:28.043619780Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1h22","title":"Add --distribute flag to ntm send for auto-distributing work from bv --robot-triage","description":"## Overview
Add `--distribute` flag to `ntm send` that automatically distributes work from `bv --robot-triage` to agents.

## Implementation
This is a convenience shorthand that internally calls `ntm assign`. The full workflow is:
1. Get recommendations from `bv --robot-triage`
2. Match recommendations to available agents
3. Send individualized prompts to each agent via `ntm send --pane=N`

## Usage
```bash
# Quick one-liner for distribution
ntm send myproject --distribute

# With options
ntm send myproject --distribute --limit=10 --cc-only
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --distribute flag parses correctly
- [ ] Calls bv --robot-triage internally
- [ ] Distributes N beads to N agents
- [ ] Logs which bead went to which agent
- [ ] Returns structured JSON in robot mode

## Dependencies
- Requires `ntm assign` command (bd-k3zq) as underlying implementation
- Requires `bv` integration (bd-55pm)

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Flag parsing
- Unit test: Distribution algorithm
- E2E test: Full distribute workflow with real agents","notes":"Closed as dropped: avoid extra entrypoints; use ntm assign or spawn --assign.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:31:55.360840482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:16:26.093871876Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T19:09:17.651720104Z","close_reason":"Dropped per canonical assign flow; redundant/overlapping entry points","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1h22","depends_on_id":"bd-55pm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:17:24.544795977Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1h22","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:17:21.177572512Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-1h7g2","title":"Add --stagger flag for delayed agent launches","description":"Add --stagger SECONDS flag to control delay between agent launches.

## Purpose
Prevent overwhelming MCP Agent Mail when launching many agents at once.

## Syntax
```bash
ntm spawn myproject --cc=10 --cod=10 --stagger=30
```

## CRITICAL LESSONS LEARNED
1. Default stagger should be 30 seconds (not 20) - MCP Agent Mail gets overwhelmed
2. Wait 4 seconds after launching agent before sending marching orders
3. Send Enter TWICE after marching orders to ensure submission
4. Log confirmation that Enter was sent for debugging

## Behavior
- Default: 30 seconds (changed from 0 to protect MCP Agent Mail)
- When set: wait N seconds between each agent launch
- Progress indicator showing launch status with confirmation

## Implementation
- Add --stagger flag to spawn command (default: 30)
- Modify agent launch loop to include delay
- Show progress: [5/20] Launching agent... -> Sent message + Enter (x2)

## Code Pattern
```go
// After launching agent
time.Sleep(4 * time.Second)  // Wait for agent to initialize
sendKeys(pane, marchingOrders)
time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
sendKeys(pane, "Enter")  // First Enter
time.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond)
sendKeys(pane, "Enter")  // Second Enter - CRITICAL
log.Printf("  -> Sent message + Enter (x2)")
time.Sleep(staggerSeconds)
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T08:19:03.308328088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:30:46.248833897Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1h9p","title":"Task: Implement timeline export to SVG and PNG","description":"## Overview
Enable exporting the timeline visualization to static image formats for reports and documentation.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/export/timeline.go:
   - ExportSVG(events []AgentEvent, opts ExportOptions) ([]byte, error)
   - ExportPNG(events []AgentEvent, opts ExportOptions) ([]byte, error)
   - ExportOptions: TimeRange, Width, Height, Scale, Theme

2. SVG export:
   - Generate clean SVG with rect elements for state bars
   - Include text labels for agents and time axis
   - Color-coded states matching TUI colors
   - Suitable for embedding in docs/reports

3. PNG export:
   - Render via Go image library or SVG-to-PNG
   - Support configurable resolution (1x, 2x, 3x)
   - Include legend for state colors

4. CLI command:
   - ntm timeline export --format=svg --output=timeline.svg
   - ntm timeline export --format=png --output=timeline.png --scale=2
   - Support time range filtering: --since=1h --until=now

## Implementation Notes
- Use standard library for SVG generation (text/template)
- For PNG, consider github.com/fogleman/gg or similar
- Include session metadata in export (project name, date)

## Acceptance Criteria
- SVG export produces valid, viewable SVG
- PNG export produces high-quality raster image
- Exports match TUI visualization closely
- CLI command works with all options
- Unit tests for export functions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:18.251073702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T20:29:25.053381123Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T20:29:25.053288629Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1h9p","depends_on_id":"bd-2t7n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:55.910055342Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1h9p","depends_on_id":"bd-f815","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:18.265808415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1hev","title":"Fix Agent Mail showing 'offline' when connected","description":"## Problem
The Agent Mail status always shows "offline" in the status bar even when the Agent Mail MCP server is available and connected.

## Screenshot Evidence
Status bar shows: "Mail: offline"

## Code Locations
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go - AgentMailUpdateMsg handling
- internal/agentmail/client.go - Agent Mail client connection
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/ticker.go - Ticker panel rendering mail status

## Possible Causes
1. **Connection check failing** - Health check returns false even when server is up
2. **MCP server not detected** - Agent Mail MCP tools not being discovered
3. **Async timing** - Mail status check completing before dashboard ready
4. **Configuration issue** - Agent Mail URL/path not configured correctly
5. **Error swallowed** - Connection error not surfaced to UI

## Investigation Steps
1. Check Agent Mail MCP server status directly: \`curl localhost:PORT/health\`
2. Verify MCP tool discovery in Claude Code config
3. Add logging to agentmail.IsAvailable() and agentmail.IsConnected()
4. Check for errors in AgentMailUpdateMsg handling

## Diagnostic Code
\`\`\`go
func (c *Client) CheckStatus() AgentMailStatus {
    log.Debug("checking agent mail status",
        "endpoint", c.endpoint,
        "timeout", c.timeout)
    
    available := c.ping()
    log.Debug("agent mail ping result", "available", available)
    
    if !available {
        return AgentMailStatus{
            Available: false,
            Connected: false,
            Reason: "server not responding",
        }
    }
    
    connected := c.authenticate()
    log.Debug("agent mail auth result", "connected", connected)
    
    return AgentMailStatus{
        Available: available,
        Connected: connected,
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Fix Pattern
\`\`\`go
// Render mail status with more detail
func (t *TickerPanel) renderMailStatus() string {
    if !t.mailAvailable {
        return "Mail: unavailable"
    }
    if !t.mailConnected {
        return "Mail: disconnected"
    }
    if t.mailLocks > 0 {
        return fmt.Sprintf("Mail: %d locks", t.mailLocks)
    }
    return "Mail: ready"
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shows "ready" when Agent Mail is available and connected
- [ ] Shows "disconnected" when server is up but auth fails
- [ ] Shows "unavailable" when server is down
- [ ] Shows lock count when file reservations exist
- [ ] Status updates on Agent Mail state changes

## Unit Tests
1. TestMailStatus_Available - server up shows "ready"
2. TestMailStatus_Unavailable - server down shows "unavailable"
3. TestMailStatus_Disconnected - auth failure shows "disconnected"
4. TestMailStatus_WithLocks - active locks shows count
5. TestMailStatus_Refresh - status updates on AgentMailUpdateMsg

## E2E Tests
1. Start dashboard with Agent Mail running - verify "ready"
2. Stop Agent Mail server - verify status changes to "unavailable"
3. Create file reservation - verify lock count appears
4. Invalid auth - verify "disconnected"

## Logging
- INFO: Log mail status changes
- DEBUG: Log connection check results
- DEBUG: Log lock count updates
- ERROR: Log connection failures with reason","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:09:20.163796969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:17:57.561963735Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:17:57.561872172Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hev","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:36.047093099Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1hz0","title":"Fix stale data display and refresh synchronization","description":"## Problem
Dashboard panels display stale or conflicting data because updates arrive asynchronously and aren't coordinated.

## Symptoms from Screenshot
- Fleet count "0/17" in status bar vs "17 panes" in stats bar
- Status showing "unknown" when agent is actually working
- Mail showing "offline" when connected

## Code Analysis

### Current Refresh Architecture (dashboard.go)
\`\`\`go
// Line 1338-1380: Staggered refresh intervals
if now.Sub(m.lastPaneFetch) >= m.paneRefreshInterval && !m.fetchingSession {
    // Panes refresh independently
}
if now.Sub(m.lastContextFetch) >= m.contextRefreshInterval && !m.fetchingContext {
    // Context refreshes independently  
}
// etc. for alerts, beads, mail...
\`\`\`

### Problems
1. **Independent refresh cycles** - Each data source has its own interval
2. **No generation tracking** - Can't tell if data is from same "snapshot"
3. **Async message handling** - Updates arrive in unpredictable order
4. **Derived values not updated** - Counts calculated from stale pane data

### Race Condition Example
\`\`\`
T=0:   Pane data: 17 panes
T=10:  Status bar renders: "17 panes"
T=50:  Pane fetch starts
T=100: Status bar renders: "17 panes" (stale)
T=150: New panes: 18
T=200: Stats bar renders: "18 panes" (updated)
       Status bar still shows "17 panes" until next render
\`\`\`

## Fix: Coordinated Refresh with Generation Numbers

### Data Generation Tracking
\`\`\`go
type Model struct {
    // Add generation tracking
    dataGeneration uint64
    lastDataUpdate time.Time
    
    // Group related data
    snapshot struct {
        panes        []tmux.Pane
        statuses     map[string]status.AgentStatus
        generation   uint64
        fetchedAt    time.Time
    }
}

func (m *Model) updateSnapshot(panes []tmux.Pane, statuses map[string]status.AgentStatus) {
    m.dataGeneration++
    m.snapshot = snapshot{
        panes:      panes,
        statuses:   statuses,
        generation: m.dataGeneration,
        fetchedAt:  time.Now(),
    }
    // Notify all panels of new snapshot
    m.beadsPanel.SetGeneration(m.dataGeneration)
    m.alertsPanel.SetGeneration(m.dataGeneration)
    // etc.
}
\`\`\`

### Coordinated Refresh
\`\`\`go
func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
    case DashboardTickMsg:
        if m.shouldRefresh() {
            // Fetch ALL data together
            return m, tea.Batch(
                m.fetchPanes(),
                m.fetchStatuses(),
                m.fetchContext(),
                m.fetchAlerts(),
            )
        }
        
    case AllDataFetchedMsg:
        // Update everything atomically
        m.updateSnapshot(msg.Panes, msg.Statuses)
        m.recalculateDerivedValues()
        return m, nil
}

func (m *Model) recalculateDerivedValues() {
    // Ensure all counts are from same snapshot
    m.claudeCount = 0
    m.codexCount = 0
    for _, pane := range m.snapshot.panes {
        if status, ok := m.snapshot.statuses[pane.ID]; ok {
            switch status.AgentType {
            case "claude": m.claudeCount++
            case "codex": m.codexCount++
            }
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Panel Consistency Check
\`\`\`go
func (p *Panel) View() string {
    // Skip render if data is stale
    if p.generation < p.requiredGeneration {
        return p.renderStaleIndicator()
    }
    return p.render()
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All panels show data from same generation/snapshot
- [ ] No conflicting values between status bar and panels
- [ ] Refresh cycle updates all data atomically
- [ ] "Last updated" indicator shows freshness
- [ ] Manual refresh (r key) triggers full coordinated refresh

## Unit Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestModel_DataConsistency(t *testing.T) {
    m := NewModel(testSession)
    
    // Simulate async updates
    m, _ = m.Update(PaneUpdateMsg{Panes: makePanes(10)})
    m, _ = m.Update(StatusUpdateMsg{Statuses: makeStatuses(10, "working")})
    
    // Counts should match
    if m.claudeCount + m.codexCount + m.geminiCount != len(m.panes) {
        t.Error("counts don't sum to pane total")
    }
}

func TestModel_GenerationTracking(t *testing.T) {
    m := NewModel(testSession)
    gen1 := m.dataGeneration
    
    m, _ = m.Update(AllDataFetchedMsg{...})
    gen2 := m.dataGeneration
    
    if gen2 <= gen1 {
        t.Error("generation should increment on update")
    }
}

func TestModel_StaleDataHandling(t *testing.T) {
    m := NewModel(testSession)
    m.dataGeneration = 5
    m.beadsPanel.SetGeneration(3) // Panel has old data
    
    view := m.View()
    if !strings.Contains(view, "stale") && !strings.Contains(view, "...") {
        t.Error("stale panel should show indicator")
    }
}
\`\`\`

## E2E Tests
1. Start dashboard, spawn new pane - all counts update together
2. Kill pane - status bar and panel update atomically
3. Rapid refreshes - no temporary inconsistencies visible
4. Network delay simulation - graceful staleness indication

## Logging Requirements
\`\`\`go
log.Debug("data refresh",
    "generation", m.dataGeneration,
    "pane_count", len(m.panes),
    "status_count", len(m.agentStatuses),
    "fetch_duration", time.Since(fetchStart))

log.Info("snapshot updated",
    "generation", m.dataGeneration,
    "claude", m.claudeCount,
    "codex", m.codexCount,
    "gemini", m.geminiCount)

log.Warn("stale data detected",
    "panel", panelName,
    "panel_gen", p.generation,
    "current_gen", m.dataGeneration)
\`\`\`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:10:40.003531017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:26:37.246360067Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:26:37.245319137Z","close_reason":"Fixed stale data display by migrating paneStatus entries when pane indices change. Added code to build old->new pane ID mappings and migrate status data during pane refresh.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hz0","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:41.605719498Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-avlbg","title":"DCG: Add --robot-dcg-status command","description":"Add robot mode command to report DCG status and configuration.

```json

        "command": "rm -rf /",
        "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
        "pane": "agent-1"
      }
    }

}
```

## Implementation

2. Gather DCG state from config and runtime stats
3. Format as JSON according to schema
4. Include recent blocked commands from audit log

## Testing
- Call with DCG enabled and available
- Call with DCG enabled but not available
- Call with DCG disabled","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:45:17.536129348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:00:34.381259683Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:00:34.381184612Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: internal/robot/dcg_status.go with tests, --robot-dcg-status flag in root.go handler.  {"id":"bd-ayjn","title":"EPIC: Smart Staggering for Rate Limit Avoidance","description":"**IDEA #10 - ELIMINATES COMMON FRUSTRATION (77% confidence)**

## What It Does
Intelligently time agent spawns and prompts to avoid hitting API rate limits. Learn from rate limit events and adapt timing automatically.

## Why This Matters
- Rate limits when spawning multiple agents are extremely common
- Currently: Users manually wait between spawns or deal with errors
- After: NTM handles timing automatically
- Particularly valuable for 'spawn 5+ agents' workflows

## Spawn Staggering
ntm spawn myproject --cc=5 --stagger=smart

Instead of spawning all 5 Claude agents simultaneously:
- Agent 1: immediate
- Agent 2: +15 seconds
- Agent 3: +30 seconds
- Agent 4: +45 seconds
- Agent 5: +60 seconds

## Adaptive Timing
1. Track rate limit events per provider
2. If rate limited, increase stagger interval by 50%
3. If no limits for N spawns, cautiously decrease interval
4. Learn optimal timing per provider/model combination

## Prompt Pacing
ntm send myproject --all 'Review the codebase' --pace=smart
Automatically adds delays between sends based on historical rate limit patterns

## Technical Implementation
- RateLimitTracker struct with event history
- GetOptimalDelay() returning provider-specific delays
- spawnWithStagger() with retry logic on rate limit
- Persistent storage of learned delays in .ntm/

## Default Delays by Provider
anthropic: 15s
openai: 10s
google: 8s

## Dependencies
None - can be implemented independently, low effort","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-b85i","title":"Task: Add automatic end-of-session hook","description":"## Purpose
Trigger summary generation automatically when sessions end.

## Scope
- Detect session termination (graceful exit, crash, timeout)
- Call SummarizeSession() with captured output
- Store summary in .ntm/summaries/ for recovery
- Update beads with session outcomes

## Acceptance Criteria
- Summary generated on all session end scenarios
- Summaries persisted even on crash (periodic checkpoints)
- Beads updated with completion status
- File reservations released with summary notes

## Technical Notes
- Hook into supervisor's session monitoring
- Consider background goroutine for periodic summary snapshots","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.

-{"id":"bd-bh74z","title":"Implement hard kill fallback using kill -9","description":"Implement hard kill fallback when soft exit fails.

## When to Use
When soft exit (Ctrl-C) doesn't work after 3 seconds, need to kill -9 the child process.

## Implementation
1. Get shell PID from tmux list-panes
2. Get child PID via pgrep -P <shell_pid>
3. kill -9 <child_pid>
4. Wait for shell to return to prompt

## Code Pattern
pane_pid=$(tmux list-panes -t session:1 -F '#{pane_index} #{pane_pid}' | grep '^N ' | awk '{print $2}')
child_pid=$(pgrep -P $pane_pid | head -1)
kill -9 $child_pid

## Error Handling
- If no child process: already exited, proceed
- If permission denied: return error with diagnostic info
- If shell doesn't return after kill: serious issue, return detailed error","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:01.791002696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:01.821005688Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bh74z","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:01.820978166Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-c0hy","title":"Task: Add ntm summary command","description":"## Purpose
Provide manual access to session summaries and on-demand summarization.

## Scope
- Add summary.go to internal/cli/
- Commands: ntm summary <session-id>, ntm summary --all, ntm summary --recent
- Support output formats: text, json, markdown

## Acceptance Criteria
- ntm summary shows latest session summary
- --all lists all available summaries
- --format controls output format
- Can regenerate summary from stored output

## Technical Notes
- Follow existing CLI patterns
- Consider pagination for --all","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-c749y","title":"caut: Implement caut binary detection and availability check","description":"Create utility function to detect if caut binary is available on PATH.

## Requirements
1. Check if 'caut' binary exists on PATH
2. Verify caut version is compatible
3. Check if caut has any data (not first run)
4. Cache result to avoid repeated lookups

## Implementation Location
Create new file: internal/integrations/caut/detect.go

```go
package caut

import (
    "os/exec"
    "sync"
)

type CautStatus struct {
    Available bool
    Version   string
    HasData   bool
    Providers []string
}

var (
    once   sync.Once
    status CautStatus
)

func GetStatus() CautStatus {
    once.Do(detect)
    return status
}

func detect() {
    path, err := exec.LookPath("caut")
    if err != nil {
        return
    }
    // Run 'caut status --json' and parse
    // Populate status struct
}
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:57:48.022783517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:30:31.858338618Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:30:31.858291790Z","close_reason":"Implemented caut binary detection with provider and usage data retrieval","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c749y","depends_on_id":"bd-2lsyy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:57:48.095691034Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
